title: Milestones are the primary declarative-organizing anchor; work cascades from + verifies upward to them; aspirational → satisfied → archived lifecycle
context: User direction 2026-05-14 dialogue articulating the substrate-canon model: a MILESTONE is the aspirational end-state-with-criterion+evidence_query authored declaratively at the top of a roadmap. Everything below it is decomposition: research (R-NNNN) explores what's needed; decisions (DEC-NNNN) settle choices; framework-gaps (FGAP-NNN) capture deficiencies blocking it; features (FEAT-NNN) scope coherent bodies of work toward it; phases group + sequence work-units; stories/tasks decompose features atomically down to atomic Task-1 execution. Verifications (VER-NNN) accumulate upward; when milestone.evidence_query satisfies against VER items, milestone moves from aspirational → satisfied (project history). Milestone is what reveals + organizes the work needing to be done. This decision crystallizes the canon already implicit in roadmap.schema.json + roadmap-plan.ts + DEC-0017 work-unit context composition.
decision: Milestones are the primary declarative-organizing anchor of work in pi-context substrate. Milestone authoring is the entry point to a body of work — the milestone names the aspirational end-state + evidence_query that defines satisfaction. All work-altitude blocks (research / decisions / framework-gaps / features / stories / phases / tasks) cascade from milestone authoring as decomposition: a milestone's evidence_query implies what work-units must produce evidence; authoring those work-units is the decomposition. Milestones bear lifecycle: aspirational/planned → active (work in progress) → satisfied (evidence_query passes; becomes project history) → archived. Roadmap is the named, status-bearing curation of an ordered milestone set; once milestones + phases are top-level blocks (FGAP-053 + FGAP-049 cascade), roadmap thins to {id, title, description, status} + lens-spec for grouping + closure-table edges for ordering. Forward verification (milestone evidence_query → satisfaction) is automatic via roadmap-plan; backward decomposition (milestone → required work-units) is human/LLM authored — framework provides scaffold, authoring fills.
consequences: Milestone elevation per FGAP-053 becomes higher-priority — milestone block-kind is the canonical entry surface for the substrate canon, not an inline roadmap detail.
Roadmap thins to envelope per FGAP-055 — substantive structure migrates to milestones + phases + relations.json.
Context-contract for unit_kind=milestone needed per FGAP-054 — gather-execution-context becomes the primitive for "what's the picture around MILESTONE-NNN".
Work-altitude tiers (FGAP-052 config.layers consumed-at-runtime gap) cascade from milestones: layer = altitude at which work-units sit; milestones organize across altitudes.
Forward query "is MILESTONE-NNN satisfied" works via existing roadmap-plan evidence_query primitive.
Backward query "what must happen before MILESTONE-NNN" works via walk-ancestors on relations.json edges once edges are authored.
Auto-decomposition (milestone → required tasks derivation) is NOT enabled by this decision; decomposition remains authoring work. Could be filed as separate framework-gap if desired.
Project goals (project.json goals[]) sit ABOVE milestones — multi-milestone arcs serve project goals; future cascade rule may make this explicit via project_goal_served_by_milestone relation_type.

references.label: Roadmap schema with milestones[] inline + evidence_query
